People completely depend on God in all things; when He withdraws His help, even the most brave and determined cannot find their strength or ability, as daily experience shows.

Jesse did not think of sending his son to the army at that critical time; but God wisely arranges actions and affairs so as to serve His designs. In times of general formality and lukewarmness, every degree of zeal which implies readiness to go further, or to risk more in the cause of God than others, will be blamed as pride and ambition, and by no one more than by close relatives, like Eliab, or negligent superiors. It was a trial of David's meekness, patience, and constancy. He had right and reason on his side, and did not retaliate with insults; with a gentle answer he turned away his brother’s anger. This victory over his own passion was more honorable than that of Goliath. Those who undertake great and public services must not be surprised if they are spoken ill of and opposed by those from whom they expect support and assistance. They must humbly continue their work, in the face not only of enemies' threats but also of friends’ slights and suspicions.

A shepherd boy, having come that very morning from tending sheep, had more courage than all the mighty men of Israel. Thus God often sends good words to His Israel, and does great things for them, by the weak and foolish things of the world. As he had answered his brother’s anger with meekness, so David answered Saul's fear with faith. When David kept sheep, he proved himself very careful and gentle toward his flock. This reminds us of Christ, the good Shepherd, who not only risked but laid down His life for the sheep. Our experience should encourage us to trust in God and be bold in the way of duty. He who has delivered will continue to do so. David gained permission to fight the Philistine. Not being used to armor like Saul’s put upon him, he was not satisfied to go that way; this was from the Lord, so that it would more clearly appear that he fought and conquered in faith, and that the victory came from Him who works by the weakest and most despised means and instruments. It is not important how excellent anything is, but how proper. Let Saul’s coat be ever so rich, and his armor ever so strong, what benefit does it bring to David if they fit him not? But faith, prayer, truth, and righteousness; the whole armour of God, and the mind that was in Christ; are equally needful for all the servants of the Lord, whatever may be their work.

The security and presumption of fools destroy them. Nothing can surpass the humility, faith, and piety which appear in David's words. He expressed his assured expectation of success; he glorified his humble appearance and arms so that the victory might be ascribed to the Lord alone.

See how fragile and uncertain life is, even when a person thinks himself best protected; how quickly, how easily, and by how small a matter, the way may be opened for life to go out and death to enter! Let not the strong man glory in his strength, nor the armed man in his armor. God resists the proud and pours contempt on those who defy Him and His people. No one ever hardened his heart against God and prospered. The history is recorded so that all may exert themselves for the honor of God and the support of His cause with bold and unwavering reliance on Him. There is one conflict in which all followers of the Lamb are, and must be engaged; one enemy, more formidable than Goliath, still challenges the armies of Israel. But resist the devil, and he will flee from you. Go forth to battle with David's faith, and the powers of darkness shall not stand against you. But how often is the Christian defeated through an evil heart of unbelief!
